title: C2 Beacon Callback
briefing: Sysmon on $TARGET shows a process in an unusual path (C:\Users\Public) making repeated outbound TCP 443 connections to the same external IP $DST1 at a regular cadence. Steady, periodic callbacks to one external host from a non-browser process are command-and-control beaconing. Identify the C2 endpoint and the pattern.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100150 / 100151 (Sysmon EID 3). Group by data.win.eventdata.destinationIp — one IP, repeated at a regular interval.

questions.prompt: What does this traffic pattern indicate?
questions.explain: Regular, periodic connections from one non-browser process to a single external IP = command-and-control beacon.

questions.prompt: What is the C2 / destination IP?
Which detail most supports a beacon (vs. normal traffic)?
options: Regular fixed interval to one IP from C:\Users\Public\svchost.exe
questions.answer: Regular fixed interval to one IP from C:\Users\Public\svchost.exe
questions.explain: Beacon jitter aside, periodic same-destination callbacks from a masquerading binary in a user-writable path is the tell.
T1071.001 Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols (C2 over HTTPS).

options: Block the C2 IP at the perimeter
Identify + kill the beaconing process (C:\Users\Public path)
Threat-intel the C2 IP for attribution
answer: Block the C2 IP at the perimeter
Identify + kill the beaconing process (C:\Users\Public path)
Threat-intel the C2 IP for attribution
questions.explain: Sinkhole the C2, isolate the host, kill the implant, and enrich the IP to find scope + campaign.
